text.title: django-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Writing a test function that accesses the database without the @pytest.mark.django_db marker.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the marker, pytest-django blocks all DB access. The test fails with "Failed: Database access not allowed". Always add @pytest.mark.django_db to any test that creates or reads model instances. Module-level pytestmark = pytest.mark.django_db applies to all tests in the module.

failure-mode.description: Patching Model.objects.filter or Model.objects.get with MagicMock to avoid DB access in service tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mocking the ORM tests the mock, not the code. The mock always returns what you tell it, so the test passes even if the service has a broken queryset. Real DB tests with @pytest.mark.django_db and savepoint rollback are fast enough — use them. Over-mocked tests break when QuerySet chaining order changes.

failure-mode.description: Patching apps.emails.utils.send_mail instead of apps.orders.services.send_mail — patching where the function is defined rather than where it is used.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Python's patch() replaces a name in a module's namespace. If orders.services does from emails.utils import send_mail, the reference is already bound. Patching emails.utils.send_mail has no effect. Patch the use site: patch('apps.orders.services.send_mail').

failure-mode.description: Using @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True) on all tests as a default instead of only for tests that specifically test on_commit behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: transaction=True requires full table truncation after each test instead of savepoint rollback — 5-10x slower. A test suite of 500 tests using transaction=True adds minutes to CI for no benefit. Use it only for code paths relying on connection.on_commit(), post-transaction signals, or Celery task visibility.
